title: Q3 Quiz 2 Draft: Materials, Terrain Assets, Modular Kits, Composition, and Process Evidence

questions.text: What is the main job of landscape materials in a castle environment?
choices: To make terrain zones readable and believable.
questions.correctAnswer: To make terrain zones readable and believable.
questions.explanation: Materials help viewers understand paths, cliffs, grass, and other terrain surfaces.

questions.text: What makes an imported terrain asset support the scene?
choices: It matches scale, style, and story purpose.
questions.correctAnswer: It matches scale, style, and story purpose.
questions.explanation: Imported assets should serve the environment rather than replace design decisions.

questions.text: Why are modular castle pieces useful?
choices: They allow repeated pieces to create larger structures efficiently.
They make all pieces unique automatically.
They prevent the need for saving.
They remove the need for composition.
questions.correctAnswer: They allow repeated pieces to create larger structures efficiently.
questions.explanation: Modular kits make construction faster while still requiring variation and planning.

questions.text: What does silhouette help communicate in a castle composition?
choices: The overall readable shape of the castle from a distance.
The audio level of a clip.
questions.correctAnswer: The overall readable shape of the castle from a distance.
questions.explanation: A strong silhouette helps the castle read clearly before small details are visible.

questions.text: Identify the term: reusable pieces assembled to build a larger structure.
questions.explanation: A modular kit contains pieces that can be combined into larger readable forms.

questions.text: Identify the term: the main visual shape or outline of an object or building.
questions.explanation: Silhouette is the readable outer shape that helps viewers identify the castle.

questions.text: A student downloads ten terrain assets but the scene looks cluttered and inconsistent. What should they do?
questions.correctAnswer: They should choose fewer assets, check scale/style, place them with purpose, and document why each supports the castle environment.
questions.explanation: Asset use should support design rather than create random clutter.

questions.text: A modular castle wall repeats the same piece so obviously that it looks artificial. What can improve it?
questions.correctAnswer: The student can vary height, spacing, rotation, damage/detail pieces, surrounding props, or camera composition while keeping the structure organized.
questions.explanation: Variation makes modular construction feel more believable.

questions.text: Put the modular castle workflow in order: review kit, block silhouette, assemble pieces, add variation, record progress.
questions.correctAnswer: 1. Review kit pieces. 2. Block the castle silhouette. 3. Assemble modular pieces. 4. Add variation. 5. Record progress evidence.
questions.explanation: The castle should move from broad readable form to more varied detail.

questions.text: In 2-3 sentences, explain how material, asset, and modular choices can support the final documentary.
questions.correctAnswer: A strong answer explains that each choice shows how the castle changed over time and why the environment became more readable, believable, or story-driven. It should mention screenshots, recordings, or production log evidence.
questions.explanation: This checks whether students connect production choices to documentary evidence.
